reviews.review_msg: Excellent, engaging prof. He has a different teaching style and approaches migration from all perspectives. He doesn't use tech but is a great lecturer. Took his class 4 years ago and still remember it to this day. Would highly recommend taking a course with him.
Great prof, really makes you think about new concepts. Short papers have helped my writing skills profusely! Take the course.

He is the most ridiculous prof ever. He thinks were still in the Mesozoic era so he doesnt use technology. Its either that or hes out to get his students. He picks the most boring readings and textbooks possible and his exams are all essay questions and things based on class discussion.

My favorite professor! Great lectures, and makes every second interesting. Though, he does not use technology and can sometimes be hard to get a hold of on email. Fair grader, and as long as you are prepared and participating his classes are really amazing.
He should be faculty.  Very passionate about the material (migrants).

Such a great prof! His "no PowerPoint rule" really makes you focus in class which ultimately makes you understand it and feel more prepared. only prof who honestly understood and cared for you. Overall, I really didn't like IS 101 as the content (arts isn't for me and it was my 1st sem so I just took it) but he genuinely makes it engaging and funny

Gregory Feldman is an amazing prof but you need to show up to class because he speaks for 2 hours and has no powerpoint slides. Content was really good and if he's your tutorial instructor  than you'll get a better mark in class. Overall he's and inspirational teacher and Id definaltely  take a class with him again.

Very charismatic and inspiring lecturer. He really makes you think. No lecture slides though so you'll have to attend class and take detailed notes of what he says. Exams were based primarily off of the weekly readings.  Also, no textbook required, which my student budget appreciated! Great prof, would highly recommend

IS itself as a course is not something I would recommend unless its something you are interested/majoring in. It is basically a history course and feldman just talks for 2 hours. Definitely teaches you how to take notes,  no slides are provided. A lot of readings that are hard to comprehend. Tutorials have a presentation but its low maintenance.

Feldman really makes you think and offers critical perspectives to the dominant paradigm. He's a great lecturer and uses movies so that you're not sitting bored for 4 hours. He's a pretty fair maker and really likes when everyone participates. Assignments are nice because it's 4 short response papers, midterm and take-home. Take his class!
